02 — WHY NOW
A Decade in the Problem.
The Solution Became Inevitable.
FAMILY BACKGROUND
Born into the supply chain. Grew up inside fashion manufacturing. Not a consultant — an insider.
10 YEARS — ADVISING BRANDS & FACTORIES
10 years advising brands and factories. Saw the same structural failures at every scale.

The Market Is Telling
Us Exactly What's Broken.
INVESTOR QUESTIONS — EARNINGS CALLS
40%+
of all problem-related questions to fashion management teams are about inventory overhang, gross margin compression, and markdown dependency — every year since 2018.
43,528 questions · 1,147 earnings calls · 10 years
INVENTORY OVERHANG GROWTH
+220%
Inventory overhang has grown over 220% in the past decade — despite massive revenue growth. Brands are getting bigger and more fragile simultaneously.
THE ROOT CAUSE
Every one of these symptoms traces back to the same failure: brands cannot see or respond to their supply chain in real time.

This is not a strategy problem. It is an infrastructure problem. Hyran is the infrastructure.

04 — ROOT CAUSE
Three Reasons Agility
Has Never Scaled.
1
BARRIER 01 — DATA
Low Bandwidth
Order-level data only. No machine-level visibility, no intra-day signals. Planning operated on information that was already hours or days old.
2
BARRIER 02 — SPEED
High Latency
Every query takes 24–48 hours. Scenario planning is impossible. By the time a demand shift reaches the mill floor, the scheduling window is gone.
3
BARRIER 03 — COMPLEXITY
Operational Complexity
Even with data and responses available, extracting actionable insight in time was beyond human capacity. Too many variables, too little time, constantly shifting demand.
"I cannot spend hours constantly updating Excel sheets."
— J.M. FABRICS, MILL
"My best people are wasting time in data entry for brands."
— SQ GROUP, MILL
THIS IS WHAT PLANNING LOOKS LIKE TODAY
MILL PLANNING — EXCEL
FACTORY ORDER TRACKING
BRAND DEMAND TABLE
EMAIL / MANUAL UPDATES
05 — THE SOLUTION
What Any Real Solution
Had to Do.
Years of development. Three non-negotiable requirements that any real solution had to meet — and that every previous attempt had failed on at least one of.
REQUIREMENT 01
Increase Bandwidth,
Reduce Latency
Machine-level data in real time — not batch updates, not manual submissions. Scenario planning is only possible at the speed of the system.
REQUIREMENT 02
Create Value for
the Factory First
Factories own the data. They must benefit independently — not just serve as a data pipe for brands. Otherwise adoption would never happen.
REQUIREMENT 03
Reduce Operational
Complexity
Once latency is solved, convert demand changes into production actions instantly — eliminating manual replanning for both factory and brand.
Previous tools failed on Requirement 02. No factory will staff perpetual data entry. Make the factory smarter first — visibility follows.
"I want to scale the business without hiring more production people to manage critical path."
— GYMSHARK, BRAND

AI-NATIVE — JSSP SOLVER FOR FABRIC MILLS
No incumbent has solved the job-scheduling problem for fabric mills. Fast React and Excel are the competition. Purpose-built AI where others use spreadsheets.
NETWORK EFFECT — ALREADY LIVE
Brands introduce Hyran to factories. Factories introduce Hyran to brands. New Balance's 52-mill pipeline is a captive install base. 50+ factories already in network.
COMPOUND DATA ADVANTAGE
Every order processed makes the AI solver more accurate. Lead-time prediction, colour commitment timing, capacity allocation — all improve with scale. The moat widens automatically.
DOMAIN MOAT — TEXTILE ENGINEERING
Fabric-specific constraints — yarn run-outs, gauge compatibility, colour changeover penalties. Cannot be replicated by a generalist SaaS team.
WHY INCUMBENTS COULDN'T BUILD THIS
Horizontal players (SAP, Oracle, o9) can't go deep enough. Fabric milling requires textile-specific constraints no generalist platform encodes.
Starting with mills looked too narrow. No incumbent pursued it. That window is closing.

APPENDIX
A1 — TECHNICAL DEEP DIVE
The NP-Hard Problem.
THE PROBLEM CLASS
Job-Shop Scheduling
Fabric mill scheduling is a variant of the Job-Shop Scheduling Problem (JSSP) — proven NP-hard. For a mill with n orders and m machines, the search space grows as (n!)^m. A mill with 20 orders and 50 machines has more possible schedules than atoms in the observable universe.
O((n!)^m)
Search space — intractable by brute force
OUR APPROACH
Proprietary Optimising Algorithm
Hyran's solver combines constraint programming with adaptive heuristics seeded from thousands of hours of real factory operation data. Near-optimal schedules generated in seconds — and continuously updated as conditions change on the floor.
~seconds
Full mill floor reoptimisation time
WHY THIS IS A MOAT
Fabric-Specific Constraints
Generic JSSP solvers exist — but they cannot handle fabric-specific constraints: yarn run-outs, colour changeover penalties, gauge compatibility, minimum batch sizes, wet-processing sequencing. Our solver encodes deep textile engineering knowledge. It cannot be replicated without both the algorithm and the domain expertise.
TSP ANALOGY
The TSP of Manufacturing
Investors familiar with the Travelling Salesman Problem will recognise the complexity class. TSP has attracted billions in research funding and powers global logistics. Fabric mill scheduling is the TSP of manufacturing — and it has never been solved commercially. Until now.
APPENDIX
A2 — FINANCING LAYER
Supply Chain Financing Layer.
THE PROBLEM
12–16% Trade Finance
Factories in Pakistan and Bangladesh face trade finance rates of 12–16% from local banks — a structural tax on every unit produced. Root cause: opacity. Banks cannot price risk accurately without real-time production data. Hyran has exactly that data.
HYRAN'S POSITION
The Risk Signal Banks Never Had
We know, in real time, what every factory in our network is producing, for whom, and when it will ship. This is the most accurate production risk signal ever generated for fashion manufacturing. It can underwrite trade finance at materially lower rates.
THE STRUCTURE
Bank Partnership Model
Phase 3 involves partnerships with DBS, Standard Chartered, and similar trade finance banks. Hyran provides real-time production data as a risk signal. Banks offer lower rates. Factories win. Hyran earns a data / facilitation fee. Margin-accretive for all parties.
SCALE POTENTIAL
$400B+ TAM
Global trade finance for fashion manufacturing exceeds $400B annually. Even a modest facilitation fee at partial penetration represents hundreds of millions in Phase 3 revenue — built entirely on the data infrastructure Hyran is laying today.
APPENDIX
A3 — COMPETITIVE ANALYSIS
Why Hyran Makes Incumbents
as Fast as Shein.
SHEIN'S EDGE
What Makes Shein Fast
Vertical integration and algorithmic demand-supply matching. They own or control the full stack — demand signal to fabric to shipping. No communication lag, no planning latency, every step automated and connected.
THE INCUMBENT PROBLEM
Why H&M / Zara Can't Match It
Legacy ERPs with no direct integration with factories cannot scenario plan at the speed necessary. They are still better than most, but cannot compete with SHEIN's speed due to SHEIN's high bandwidth, low latency communication and reduction of operational complexity through AI tools.
THE HYRAN BRIDGE
The Retrofit Solution
Hyran inserts at the fabric layer — the longest chokepoint — and connects the brand's demand signal directly to the mill's scheduling engine in real time. Shein-equivalent response at the supply chain's most critical point, with an incumbent's supplier relationships intact.
